title: FIRRTL memory read-under-write is not respected

features.name: memory read_under_write attribute with old/new semantics
features.description: Code should include a memory declaration with read-under-write attribute set to either old or new, where old returns the pre-write value and new returns the value being written during concurrent read/write to same address
features.snippet:
mem memory:
  data-type => UInt<8>[2]
  depth => 256
  read-latency => 1
  write-latency => 1
  read-under-write => old
  reader => r0
  writer => w1

features.name: dual_port_memory with conflicting read_write access
features.description: Code should include a memory with two ports (reader and writer) accessing the same address simultaneously, where the read-under-write semantics determines the returned data value
features.snippet:
input r0: {addr: UInt<8>, en: UInt<1>, clk: Clock, flip data: UInt<8>[2]}
input w1: {addr: UInt<8>, en: UInt<1>, clk: Clock, data: UInt<8>[2], mask: UInt<1>[2]}
mem memory:
  data-type => UInt<8>[2]
  depth => 256
  read-under-write => old
  reader => r0
  writer => w1
